parsed_findings.impact: Enables arbitrary SQL execution, potentially leading to data exfiltration or database compromise.
parsed_findings.proof: ?dir=asc, (SELECT 1 FROM (SELECT(SLEEP(5)))a)
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Inject a malicious SQL payload into the dir query parameter.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: The direction parameter is directly interpolated into the ORDER BY clause without validation, allowing for SQL injection.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: User input 'url' -> axios.get(url) -> response returned to user.
parsed_findings.impact: Access to internal network services and cloud metadata, leading to potential credential theft or internal reconnaissance.
parsed_findings.proof: The 'url' query parameter is passed directly to axios.get(u) on line 6 without effective hostname or protocol validation.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Request /fetch?url=http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ to retrieve sensitive instance metadata.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via unvalidated user-controlled URL in axios.get.

parsed_findings.impact: Information disclosure via error side-channel (500 vs 403) and Denial of Service.
parsed_findings.proof: Line 4 accesses inv.project_id without verifying db.get_invoice(inv_id) returned an object, causing an AttributeError if ID is invalid.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send PATCH request with non-existent inv_id to /api/invoices/<id>.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Missing existence check for fetched invoice leads to NULL pointer dereference when accessing project_id.

parsed_findings.impact: Denial of Service (DoS) for individual requests and potential stack trace disclosure.
parsed_findings.proof: Line 6 calls .get() on request.json which is None if the Content-Type is not application/json or body is empty.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send PATCH request with valid inv_id but invalid Content-Type or empty body.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Missing check for request.json existence leads to NULL pointer dereference.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: User-controlled query parameter is weakly sanitized and then used in a file system read operation.
parsed_findings.impact: An attacker can read sensitive system files or application source code, leading to full system compromise.
parsed_findings.proof: The replace('../', '') call only removes the first occurrence and can be bypassed with multiple or nested sequences.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Request /download?file=../../etc/passwd to bypass the single replacement and access the system password file.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Insufficient path traversal filter allows reading arbitrary files outside the intended directory.

parsed_findings.impact: Critical: Remote Code Execution (RCE) allows complete compromise of the application server.
parsed_findings.proof: Generate a base64-encoded pickle payload using __reduce__ to execute os.system, set it as the 'state' cookie, and request /resume.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: 1. Create malicious pickle object. 2. Base64 encode it. 3. Set 'state' cookie to this value. 4. Access /resume.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: The /resume endpoint deserializes untrusted state data from a cookie using pickle.loads, enabling arbitrary code execution.

parsed_findings.impact: Privilege escalation.
parsed_findings.proof: PATCH /api/users/1 with body {'isSupport':true} elevates privileges.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send a PATCH request with protected fields in the request body.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Mass assignment via Object.assign allows overwriting sensitive properties like isSupport.

parsed_findings.impact: Unauthorized profile modification.
parsed_findings.proof: Changing the :id parameter allows modifying arbitrary user records.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Submit a PATCH request using another user's ID in the URL.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Broken authorization logic fails to validate ownership, allowing IDOR to other users.

parsed_findings.impact: Denial of Service.
parsed_findings.proof: An invalid ID results in a TypeError when calling Object.assign on a null value.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send a PATCH request with an ID that does not exist in the database.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Lack of user existence check before Object.assign causes a crash on invalid IDs.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: User input from request.json flows to name and is executed in os.system.
parsed_findings.impact: Complete system compromise via remote code execution.
parsed_findings.proof: Providing '; id #' as name executes the id command via os.system.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST to /backup with json {'name': '; id #'}.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: The name parameter is directly interpolated into a shell command without sanitization, allowing arbitrary command execution.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: User input from request.json passes startswith check and is executed in os.system.
parsed_findings.impact: Complete system compromise via remote code execution.
parsed_findings.proof: A value of '/var/data/; id' for src satisfies the prefix check and executes the id command.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST to /backup with json {'src': '/var/data/; id'}.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Insufficient validation of the src parameter allows shell metacharacters after the required prefix to execute arbitrary commands.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Input in POST /comments is processed by markdown.markdown, stored in DB, and served unsanitized in GET /comments.
parsed_findings.impact: Arbitrary JavaScript execution in users' browsers, leading to session theft or unauthorized actions.
parsed_findings.proof: Payload `{"body": "<script>alert(1)</script>"}` results in stored script execution.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Post malicious HTML in the body field to /comments, then fetch the comments list via GET to trigger execution.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Stored XSS via markdown rendering of unsanitized input saved to the database.

parsed_findings.impact: An attacker can generate infinite funds for their own account.
parsed_findings.proof: A transfer request with a negative amount increases the sender's balance due to subtraction of a negative value.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Submit a POST request to /transfer with {'amount': -1000, 'to': 'user2'} in the JSON body.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Negative amount transfer allows account balance inflation.

parsed_findings.impact: Users can spend more than their actual balance, leading to financial loss for the platform.
parsed_findings.proof: Concurrent requests read the same balance before updates occur, allowing multiple transfers exceeding the limit.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Use a script to send 10 concurrent transfer requests each for the total account balance.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: TOCTOU race condition allows double-spending.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Verification fails on forged token -> catch block executes -> production check passes -> unverified decoded payload is returned to caller.
parsed_findings.impact: Complete authentication bypass and privilege escalation by allowing attackers to spoof identity and permissions.
parsed_findings.proof: Forcing a verification error with a forged JWT in production triggers the catch block which returns the unverified decoded payload.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Submit a JWT with modified claims and an invalid signature to the service when NODE_ENV is set to production.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Authentication bypass via unverified JWT decoding in production when signature verification fails.

parsed_findings.impact: An attacker can bypass authentication and trigger the webhook processing with arbitrary data.
parsed_findings.proof: The use of `==` for comparing the signature `got` with the calculated `want` allows for a timing attack.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Submit multiple requests with different signatures and measure the response time to reconstruct the valid HMAC.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Timing attack via non-constant-time HMAC comparison.

parsed_findings.impact: Attackers can cause duplicate actions or state changes by replaying previously valid webhook events.
parsed_findings.proof: The signature verification does not include a timestamp or nonce, allowing captured requests to be replayed.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Intercept a valid request and resubmit it to the server; it will be processed successfully again.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Insecure webhook implementation lacks replay protection.

parsed_findings.impact: Attackers can redirect users to malicious sites for phishing.
parsed_findings.proof: ?next=//evil.com satisfies startswith('/') but redirects to an external domain.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Access /go?next=//evil.com and observe the Location header in the response.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Open redirect via protocol-relative URLs starting with // bypasses the / validation.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Craft malicious XML with DTD entity; POST to /xml/import; parser resolves file path; content is returned in title field.
parsed_findings.impact: Arbitrary file read or Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via malicious XML external entity resolution.
parsed_findings.proof: parser = etree.XMLParser(resolve_entities=True, no_network=False)
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Post XML with an external entity: <!DOCTYPE x [<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM 'file:///etc/passwd'>]> <title>&xxe;</title>
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: The XML parser is configured to resolve external entities and allow network access, enabling XML External Entity (XXE) injection.

parsed_findings.impact: The process will terminate unexpectedly due to a segmentation fault or access violation.
parsed_findings.proof: The condition 'if (out || out_sz == 0)' is satisfied when 'out' is a valid pointer, but allows execution to continue to line 5 when 'out' is NULL.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Pass a NULL pointer for the 'out' argument and a positive value for 'out_sz' to the copy_name function.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: The function incorrectly returns if 'out' is non-NULL, ensuring it only proceeds when 'out' is NULL, which causes a null pointer dereference in memcpy.
